calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Orders: 1)Compelling Trustee To Produce Trust Records; 2) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Temporary Successor Trustee 3) Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Surcharging Trustee 5) Denying Trustee Compensation; And 6) Awarding Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioners
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel and Azita Rahim appeared by zoom for Catherine Gill and for and with Thomas Gill, Jr. (in person).
Evan Abrams and Alex Reese appeared by zoom for Susan Casey (trustee).

The court informed the parties that it deems the petitioners' response to the order to show cause as being timely and that it makes the ex parte request moot. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the issue of whether or not it is appropriate for the court to dismiss this petition, whether new case law applies to this trust and whether or not the path forward requires a different petition. The matters are under submission with the court's intent to follow through with the order to show cause and dismiss the petition with prejudice. The court informed the parties that it may issue its own order, or that it may use the order that was provided.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2024 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Orders: 1)Compelling Trustee To Produce Trust Records; 2) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Temporary Successor Trustee 3) Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Surcharging Trustee 5) Denying Trustee Compensation; And 6) Awarding Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioners
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Continued To Jan-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Gill in propria persona.
Cathy Gill appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Alex Reese and Evan Abrams appeared by zoom for Susan Casey (respondent/trustee).

The court noted that the special trustee has taken action and is proposing that this petition be dismissed and deemed as complete resolution of the disputes. Mr. Gill stated that he is not in agreement with that. The parties presented oral argument to support their positions as to why the court should/should not dismiss the petition, whether the special trustee's ruling is binding, whether the provision in the trust regarding "resolution of disputes" is supposed to operate the same as a binding arbitration agreement, etc. The court will issue an order to show cause regarding submission of a brief as to why the court should not dismiss the petition. The brief is to be filed and served on or before December 4, 2023. A response by Mr. Reese is to be filed on or before December 18, 2023. A reply is to be filed by January 8, 2024. The Petition for Orders Compelling Trustee to Produce Trust Documents, etc. is continued to January 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to show cause re dismissal will also be set for January 22, 2024.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Orders: 1)Compelling Trustee To Produce Trust Records; 2) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Temporary Successor Trustee 3) Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Surcharging Trustee 5) Denying Trustee Compensation; And 6) Awarding Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioners
Off Calendar Jul-24-2023 Continued To Oct-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Alex Reese appearing via zoom for Susan Gill Casey (trustee).
Thomas Gill Jr. appearing in person (pro per) and Cathy Gill appearing via zoom (pro per).

Discussion was held as to the special co-trustee that discovery has not taken place, that the limited information Mr. Spector has on this matter Mr. Gill is requiring more time to prepare for a complete discovery, and that he has full confidence in Mr. Spector. Petition for orders etc., is continued to October 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties to file a joint status supplement by October 16, 2023.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: Ateesh Datt: Deputy: Singh: Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Orders: 1)Compelling Trustee To Produce Trust Records; 2) Suspending Trustee And Appointing Temporary Successor Trustee 3) Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee 4) Surcharging Trustee 5) Denying Trustee Compensation; And 6) Awarding Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioners
Off Calendar Apr-25-2023 Continued To Jul-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Sasha Collins and Devin Picciano appeared by zoom for Thomas M. Gill Jr present by zoom and Catherine Gill present by zoom.
Alex Reese and Evan Abrams appearing via zoom for and with Susan Casey appearing via zoom.

The Court asked status regarding Ms. Gill/trustee. Mr. Reese stated that Mrs. Gill has not been properly served, that they are discussing the issue of having a Guardian Ad Litem and that Mrs. Gill lacks mental capacity to make decisions for herself. Discussion was held regarding the terms of the trust with respect to the appointment of a successor trustee, the issue of whether or not notice of the supplement is appropriate/adequate, whether this petition should be withdrawn and allow the parties to work things out according to what is outlined in the trust, whether it is appropriate for the Court to appoint the Dan Sepctor as a successor trustee, the issue of having an interim special co-trustee in place, the scope of what the special co-trustee would be doing, the expense that will be incurred in litigation, etc. The petition for orders compelling trustee to produce trust records, etc., is under submission. Mr. Reese is to provide a proposed order indicating why the petition should be taken off calendar. Mrs. Collins is to provide a proposed order finding that notice is sufficient and appointing Dan Spector as the special co-trustee. The parties agree that if Mr. Spector is appointed, a status report should be filed in about 60 days.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.
